bonuses.leadin: This artist painted women in pink and blue leaning over in Gather Ye Rosebuds, While Ye May. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who depicted a red-haired woman in a white dress sitting on top of a quilt while floating in a boat in one of his depictions of the Lady of Shalott.

A Waterhouse painting of this Shakespeare character shows her sitting on a tree next to a pond. Elizabeth Siddall nearly died of hypothermia while posing for a John Everett Millais portrait of this character.

Waterhouse depicted this maroon-clad character wrapping a tan scarf around a man she is about to kiss in another painting. Frank Dicksee showed this character leaning down from a black horse to kiss a knight in ornate armor.
answers: “La Belle Dame sans Merci” [accept answers indicating “the woman from ‘La Belle Dame sans Merci’ by John Keats”]
answers_sanitized: "La Belle Dame sans Merci" [accept answers indicating "the woman from 'La Belle Dame sans Merci' by John Keats"]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artist painted women in pink and blue leaning over in Gather Ye Rosebuds, While Ye May. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this artist who depicted a red-haired woman in a white dress sitting on top of a quilt while floating in a boat in one of his depictions of the Lady of Shalott.

A Waterhouse painting of this Shakespeare character shows her sitting on a tree next to a pond. Elizabeth Siddall nearly died of hypothermia while posing for a John Everett Millais portrait of this character.

Waterhouse depicted this maroon-clad character wrapping a tan scarf around a man she is about to kiss in another painting. Frank Dicksee showed this character leaning down from a black horse to kiss a knight in ornate armor.

bonuses.leadin: Name some artists inspired by Shakespeare, for 10 points each.
parts: This artist created an extremely creepy fairy retinue for A Midsummer Night’s Dream in his painting Titania and Bottom and a terrifying-looking Lady Macbeth Seizing the Daggers, but is better known for The Nightmare.

This non-Millais Pre-Raphaelite artist did many studies of Ophelia rather ominously sitting on a tree branch over a lilypad-covered river. He also branched out into Tennyson fan art with The Lady of Shalott.

This Swiss Neoclassical artist of Cornelia Pointing to Her Children as Her Treasures depicted Shakespearean actor David Garrick grasping a chair and also showed Miranda and Ferdinand in the woods in The Tempest.
